title: Annual freshwater withdrawals, domestic (% of total freshwater withdrawal)
definition: Annual freshwater withdrawals refer to total water withdrawals, not counting evaporation losses from storage basins. Withdrawals also include water from desalination plants in countries where they are a significant source. Withdrawals can exceed 100 percent of total renewable resources where extraction from nonrenewable aquifers or desalination plants is considerable or where there is significant water reuse. Withdrawals for domestic uses include drinking water, municipal use or supply, and use for public services, commercial establishments, and homes. Data are for the most recent year available for 1987-2002.

editorial.shortHook: A candidate map for domestic water withdrawals.
editorial.patternNote: The darkest countries include Brunei Darussalam, Luxembourg, Djibouti, while the lightest include Somalia, Fed. Rep., Afghanistan, Austria; that contrast is the fastest way to read the domestic water withdrawals pattern.
editorial.whyItMatters: Domestic water withdrawals may add useful WORLDPRINT variety once source coverage, unit clarity, map interest, and distractor ambiguity are reviewed.

bestProbeCountries.reason: High anchor: this country sits near the darkest end of the map.
Low anchor: this country clarifies the lightest end of the scale.
Middle check: this country helps separate a true pattern from one-off outliers.

commonConfusions.reason: Agricultural water withdrawals can tempt players because it shares a strong country ranking with this map, but its unit and definition point to a different story.
Water productivity can tempt players because it shares a strong country ranking with this map, but its unit and definition point to a different story.
Water stress lives in the same category, so it can feel plausible until the high and low countries are checked.

editorial.difficultyReason: Standard difficulty: the map has readable regional structure, but the best answer still depends on checking the unit and country anchors.
regionalSignals: Environment maps reward separating land cover, exposure, and emissions intensity.
Check both forested and arid or industrialized outliers.

reviewNotes: Batch 2 promotion: Domestic water withdrawals adds useful practice depth with an understandable unit.
